error.signature: ROS2 action goal preemption causes crash or undefined behavior when new goal arrives during execution
error.regex: (action.*preempt.*race|goal.*cancel.*crash|action.*server.*concurrent|goal.*handle.*invalid|ROS2\ action\ goal\ preemption\ causes\ crash\ or\ undefined\ behavi)

verdict.summary: ROS2 action servers don't automatically handle goal preemption. When a new goal arrives while executing a previous one, both execute concurrently unless you explicitly implement preemption logic in handle_goal and execute callbacks.

dead_ends.action: Assume new goals automatically cancel previous goals
dead_ends.why_fails: Unlike ROS1 actionlib, ROS2 action servers do NOT auto-preempt. Multiple goals execute simultaneously unless you explicitly manage goal lifecycle.

dead_ends.action: Cancel the previous goal in handle_goal callback
dead_ends.why_fails: handle_goal is called before the new goal starts executing. Canceling the old goal here creates a race condition if the old execute callback hasn't checked for cancellation yet.

workarounds.action: Use a single-goal policy: reject or queue new goals while one is executing
workarounds.how: def handle_goal(self, goal_handle): if self._executing: return GoalResponse.REJECT; return GoalResponse.ACCEPT

workarounds.action: Implement preemption with threading lock in execute callback
workarounds.how: In execute: periodically check if goal_handle.is_cancel_requested or if a new goal has been accepted. Clean up and return CANCELED.

workarounds.action: Use goal_handle.status to track and manage concurrent goals explicitly
workarounds.how: Maintain a list of active goal handles. In handle_goal, cancel previous goals and wait for their execute callbacks to finish.
